$comment: Central knobs for cost / cadence / model selection. Edit here instead of in scripts. See src/lib/survey-config.ts for the typed loader.

baseline.comment: Baseline (no news) panel = 3 stackable tiers, deduped: (1) the 5 flagship anchors (always in); (2) fillPoolCap top usage-ranked models; (3) openSourceFillCap additional open-weights models. 5 + 10 + 15 ≈ 30-model panel. Each tier is also a dashboard filter (anchors / usage / open-source).

informed.comment: Informed (with news). scope 'anchors-only' keeps the news-assisted portion cheap; 'pool' would reuse the full baseline pool. fillPoolCap only applies when scope = 'pool'.

anchors.comment: Anchors are sampled `repetitions` times per cell at `sampleTemperature` so we can show an answer DISTRIBUTION (with uncertainty), not a single argmax. Draw #0 is reasoned (for the displayed rationale); draws #1..N are answer-only and cheap. Fill models stay at 1 deterministic draw. Repetition applies to closed questions only (open free-text stays at 1).

registry.comment: How many usage-ranked text-generation families update-models keeps active (in addition to anchors). Kept large so the active pool holds enough open-weights models to fill the open-source tier (fillPoolCap + openSourceFillCap).

models.comment: Cheap models for the autonomous summarizer/classifier/digest so cost stays low. digestAuthor null = first model in the run roster (a flagship). The report/digest is written by a cheap open-source model (gpt-oss-120b).

backfill.comment: Fills the dashboard back over `days`, one run every `stepDays` (matches the ongoing ~twice-weekly cadence). The flagship news (informed) diets run every `informedStepDays`-th run using real historical headlines (NewsAPI/GDELT) for that day. days = how far back.
